name: Ombria del Benicadell Trail (PR-CV 213)
name_original: La Ombría del Benicadell (PR-CV 213) — Circular desde Beniatjar
description: Hike the difficult 10.2-mile (16.4 km) Shade of Benicadell (PR-CV 213) circular trail from Beniatjar, gaining 2302 feet (702 metres).
region_intro: Hiking around Beniatjar offers access to the Benicadell mountain range, which rises to 1,104 meters and forms a natural divide between the Vall d'Albaida and El Comtat. The landscape features agricultural terraces with olive groves, providing a foreground to the dramatic mountain crag. Diverse Mediterranean ecosystems include mature pine forests, holm oak groves, and scrubland rich in aromatic plants. Best hiking trails around Beniatjar The most popular hiking route is Ombria del Benicadell Trail (PR-CV 213), a 10.2 miles (16.4 km) difficult trail that takes 6 hours 3 minutes to complete. This circular route offers views of La Ombría del Benicadell. Another top favourite among local hikers is Benicadell Ice House – Benicadell Summit loop from Beniatjar, a difficult 7.7 miles (12.4 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain and passes the historic Benicadell Icehouse. Local hikers also love the Benicadell Summit – View of Alt de Benicadell loop from Beniatjar, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail leading through the lower slopes of the Benicadell mountain, often completed in about 2 hours 51 minutes. Hiking around Beniatjar is defined by the Benicadell mountain range, agricultural terraces, and diverse Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate ascents to challenging summit routes. The routes in Beniatjar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 300 reviews. More than 2,200 hikers have used komoot to explore Beniatjar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Beniatjar?
A: There are over 90 hiking routes to explore around Beniatjar, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a wide selection, from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Beniatjar?
A: Yes, Beniatjar offers 8 easy routes suitable for beginners or families. These trails typically explore the lower slopes and agricultural terraces, providing a gentler experience of the landscape. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33867535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benicadell Summit – View of Alt de Benicadell loop from Beniatjar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.5 km trail that can be completed in under 3 hours, offering beautiful views without extreme difficulty.

Q: What are the most challenging hikes in the Beniatjar area?
A: For experienced hikers, the Benicadell mountain range offers 25 difficult routes. These often involve significant elevation gain, such as the ascent to the Benicadell summit. A notable challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e818892494/ombria-del-benicadell-trail-pr-cv-213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ombria del Benicadell Trail (PR-CV 213)&lt;/a&gt;, a 16.4 km circular route with over 700 meters of elevation gain, taking around 6 hours.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Beniatjar?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Beniatjar are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e818892494/ombria-del-benicadell-trail-pr-cv-213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ombria del Benicadell Trail (PR-CV 213)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10350537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benicadell Ice House – Benicadell Summit loop from Beniatjar&lt;/a&gt;, which includes historical landmarks.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The trails around Beniatjar showcase diverse Mediterranean ecosystems. You'll encounter agricultural terraces with ancient olive groves, mature pine forests, small holm oak groves, and scrubland rich in aromatic plants like rosemary and sage. The dramatic Benicadell mountain crag provides a stunning backdrop to many routes.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the area is rich in history. Hikers can discover the ancient Benicadell Icehouse along routes leading to the summit. The remains of the 12th-century Muslim castle, Castillo de Carbonera, are also accessible via hiking trails. Additionally, the Coves del Castellet feature Bronze Age rock art, offering a glimpse into the region's ancient past.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Beniatjar?
A: The hiking trails in Beniatjar are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning views of the Benicadell mountain range, the diverse landscapes, and the well-maintained paths.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Beniatjar?
A: Beniatjar benefits from a Mediterranean climate. While rains typically occur from autumn to spring, hiking is generally pleasant for much of the year. For summit ascents, spring and autumn offer milder temperatures. Winter hiking is possible, but higher elevations of Benicadell may experience snowfall in January and February.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest or viewpoints to look out for?
A: Absolutely. Along the trails, you can find several notable viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3174179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Alt de Benicadell&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3953272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Benicadell&lt;/a&gt; offer panoramic vistas of the surrounding landscape. The &lt;a href="https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beniatjar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Fontetes Recreational Area&lt;/a&gt; is also a good spot for a break with a fountain during climbs.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Beniatjar?
A: While specific parking details for each trailhead vary, Beniatjar is a village that serves as a common starting point for many routes. Parking is generally available within or on the outskirts of the village, providing access to the Benicadell mountain trails.

Q: Can I find any wild swimming spots near the hiking trails?
A: While Beniatjar is primarily known for its mountain and forest trails, the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/210458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beniarrés Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; could offer opportunities for water activities, though it's not directly on the hiking routes from Beniatjar. Always check local regulations regarding swimming.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking?
A: The diverse Mediterranean ecosystems around Beniatjar support various wildlife. Hikers might spot foxes and wild boars, particularly in the more secluded forest areas. The region is also home to a variety of bird species.
tips: The activity of artificial cellars has been known since Roman times. Their development took place between the 16th and 19th centuries and they were used until the middle of the 19th century, when, with the appearance of electrical appliances, they fell into disuse.
Beautiful views from the Alt del Benicadell and the geodesic point.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Shade of Benicadell circular hike?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered difficult, requiring a good level of physical fitness. The route is approximately 16.4 km long with an elevation gain of around 700m. Be prepared for some scrambling, especially in the last 150 meters to the summit, which requires sure-footedness and sturdy footwear.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The northern orientation of the ascent means it often provides shade until midday, making it a good option during warmer months. However, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Always check the weather forecast for the Benicadell mountain before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this hike in Beniatjar?
faqs.answer: The circular route typically starts from the village of Beniatjar. You should be able to find parking within the village itself, near the trailhead. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked for hikers.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and views along the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers impressive views throughout. Key highlights include the View of Alt de Benicadell, the historic Benicadell Ice House, and the rewarding Benicadell Summit, which provides panoramic vistas extending to the Alicante coastline and even Ibiza on clear days. You'll also pass through charming agricultural landscapes and forests.

faqs.question: Is the Shade of Benicadell trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in the region are dog-friendly, the challenging nature of this particular route, including sections requiring scrambling, might be difficult for some dogs. If you bring your dog, ensure they are fit enough for the terrain and always keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas and near wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Sierra del Benicadell Protected Landscape?
faqs.answer: The Sierra del Benicadell is a Protected Landscape, but typically, no specific permits or entrance fees are required for hiking on established trails like this one. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. You'll start on tracks from Beniatjar, transition to zigzagging forest paths on the north face, and encounter wider tracks and single-track trails. Near the summit, the terrain becomes rockier, requiring some hand use and minor scrambling along the ridge. You'll also pass through agricultural areas with olive groves.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: This circular hike is estimated to take over 6 hours to complete, given its distance of approximately 16.4 km and significant elevation gain. This duration does not include extended stops for breaks or enjoying the views.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to rest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, you'll find a pleasant area called Les Fontetes, which has tables, benches, and a fountain, making it an ideal spot for a rest. The Casa Forestal de Les Planisses is another forest house along the route that could serve as a point of interest or a potential alternative starting point for a shorter hike.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Camino del Alba, El Camí IT5TR5 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, Pista Forestal de l'Ombria de Benicadell, Camí de la Serra, l'Escaló, and PRV 213.4.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' grading, length, elevation gain, and sections requiring scrambling, this trail is generally not recommended for young children or families seeking an easy outing. It's best suited for experienced hikers with a good level of fitness.
